_README: Human attribution key for VALIDATING the A2 citation checker (litstream/eval/citation_check.py). One JSONL row per claim<->citation pair, hand-labeled by a domain expert from the synthesis + the cited *_evidence.md. The checker is run over the SAME pairs and its predicted labels are scored against the 'gold' field here (precision / recall / Cohen's κ) — this is how we learn whether to trust the checker before acting on its verdicts. Target ~50 pairs; sample across all four labels and all cited papers, not just the easy Supportive ones. Copy to attribution_key.jsonl and fill in. Fields: id; claim (the sentence/clause as it appears in the synthesis); citation (the raw [Author Year] marker); evidence_file (the resolved *_evidence.md from Appendix A); gold (one of Supportive | Partial | Contradictory | Irrelevant); note (why — the supporting/contradicting span or the reason it's partial/irrelevant).

claim: surface protein outperforms RNA for fine-grained immune subtyping and rare-cell detection
note: benchmark shows protein/integrated F1 ~0.95-1.0 at <=1% rarity vs RNA ~0.70-0.90

claim: totalVI is the #1 multimodal integration method
note: Yin ranks totalVI top for integration across 10 datasets

claim: totalVI decontamination removes negative ADT peaks, harming rare-cell threshold-gating
note: Zheng's 14-method normalization benchmark documents neg-peak loss under decontamination

claim: totalVI is the #1 multimodal integration method
note: Zheng reports totalVI failed multi-center integration where ADTnorm succeeded — opposite of the claim (illustrates a contradiction pair)

claim: predicted proteins carry ~8-25% error and can fabricate signal
note: scProTrans paper reports prediction error in this band

claim: scAIDE is the best clustering method for rare cells
note: Yin shows scAIDE top F1 at 0.5-1% but FlowSOM most robust — 'best' is task-dependent, only partly supported

claim: classical monocytes are ~85% of total monocytes
note: numeric frequency claim — VERIFY the number is literally in the source; entailment models are weak here

claim: benchmarks were tested at 2k-10k cells while this project targets 10^5-10^6
note: the 2k-10k figure is supported; the project-scale half is project context, not from the paper

claim: percent-positivity beats fold-change for rare markers
note: this is a [Zheng]/percent-positivity finding; the scProTrans paper does not address percent-positivity vs fold-change — example of a mis-attributed citation the checker should catch
